_about: Named Hindu festival dates for the Today-screen greeting (Happy X) + the Panchang month markers. THIS IS DATA ONLY — edit this file, never the code. The loader is shared/astro/festivals.py.
_source: Cross-checked against drikpanchang.com (the reference almanac). Dates can vary +/-1 day by region/tradition; use the common all-India date.
_how_to_update: Once a year (around December, when the next year's dates are finalized): open drikpanchang.com, and add a new "YYYY": [ ... ] block below, listing that year's major JOYFUL festivals (only ones you'd wish someone 'Happy X'). Each entry = { "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "name": "Festival Name" }. Optional "greeting" field overrides the default 'Happy <name>'. Keep the list sorted by date. Then bump _last_updated and _status. Full guide: FESTIVALS.md.

_status.2026: populated (starter set — please VERIFY each date against drikpanchang.com; a few festivals shift +/-1 day by tradition)
_status.2027: NOT YET ADDED — add in Dec 2026 from drikpanchang.com once finalized (the loader simply shows no greeting for any year that's missing, so nothing breaks)
